figures.caption: Figure 2: Seed-averaged daily portfolio equity curves of the ten LLM agents and CSI300 buy-and-hold (dashed grey) under blinded × open-research on the long 2024-01-01 to 2026-04-10 window, starting from CNY 1,000,000.
Figure 3: Per-attacker recovery under blinded on tk@1, tk@5, date ±7d, and joint success (sqrt-scaled). Grey dashed: random baseline.

Figure 1: Overview of KTD-FIN. An LLM agent receives CSI300 features under one of four data-side masking conditions (bright, stock-blind, date-blind, blinded), executes a research-then-submission loop, and submits a JSON action that is run by a Qlib-based stock-market executor with realistic constraints.
Table 9: Complete leaderboard for all eighteen trained Qlib factor models on the long 2024-01-01 to 2026-04-10 window, sorted by total return. CSI300 buy-and-hold inserted at its rank position.

Table 4: Headline leaderboard, sorted by total return; the shaded CSI300 row sits at its rank position in each panel. α-rk ranks LLMs by Barra-attributed selection alpha; bold marks the only agent with α ≥ 0.
Table 6: Six read-only tools under open-research. The agent may invoke any tool any number of times within a step.

Table 12: De-anonymization attacker probe under the full masked payload (the exact view the agent receives in blinded mode). Each attacker sees 200 randomly drawn probes; rates are over successful responses only. Top-1 and joint success are the headline numbers (Section 4.3); board accuracy is intentionally high (board field unmasked) and serves as a sanity check.
Table 2: Ten regime-stratified evaluation windows. †W10 is strictly post-cutoff for the anchor model.
